Title: Support "social authentication" via Keycloak
Body: Allow sign in using populare social media accounts

Title: Add to bulk importer the ability to delete all references from a collection
Body: This single request deletes all references in a collection: ``` DELETE /orgs/KenyaMOH/collections/KenyaEMR/references/ {     "references": ["*"] } ```  Bulk importer could do this: ``` {"__method": "DELETE", "owner": "KenyaMOH", "owner_type": "Organization", "collection": "KenyaEMR", "references": ["*"]} ```

Title: Allow source/collection admins to control visibility of repo versions separate from tags
Body: Right now, the visibility of a source/collection version is tied somewhat to its `released` and `retired` flags. This is especially true when using the `latest` keyword (which evaluates to "latest released version") or when evaluating a version-less reference, like `/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/concepts/1234/`. We want administrators to have complete control over how OCL resolves these pointers, which means letting them set repo version visibility / behavior separately from flags.  In addition, we want to support the rest of the "flags" supported by the FHIR `status` field for CodeSystem, ValueSet, ConceptMap (draft | active | retired | unknown). Here's the publication status ValueSet: https://www.hl7.org/fhir/valueset-publication-status.html

Title: Implement access control for exports
Body: Right now, a source or collection is either public or private, and this access control applies to all aspects of that repository. From the perspective of a standards development organization, reference access (searching/browsing) is treated separately from distribution.  How could we allow admins to control reference access to a source/collection separately from distribution access?  For now, we can keep these definitions simple: * Reference - search, browse, viewing details of resources, basic API access (though we should probably put a ceiling to the `limit` parameter) * Distribution - users may download full exports  Open questions: * How do we (and should we) limit mappings and references  @bmamlin FYI

Title: Automatically generate changelog for OCL primary repos
Name_type Standardization
Body: Standardize the format of the name_type attribute in OCL.   For example, we are seeing name_type attributes that are "Fully Specified" ([link](https://api.qa.openconceptlab.org/orgs/PEPFAR-Test10b/sources/MER/concepts/p1OMag94BGr/?verbose=true)) and name_type attributes that are "FULLY_SPECIFIED" ([link](https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/OMRSCOVIDSquad/collections/OMRSCOVIDVaccination/concepts/166356/?verbose=true)).   Note that OpenMRS Dictionary Manager will be slightly affected by it, so we should coordinate with that team when we start to implement these changes. ![image.png](https://images.zenhubusercontent.com/5df114a3d094b314eeec671c/c68cc5a5-339b-4d35-8ef6-29b524389514)

Title: Improve memory management for import tasks
Body: Currently the whole import file is stored in memory and passed around to workers. It uses memory on Redis, but also in all individual workers processing it i.e. main worker and concurrent workers.   Things to consider: 1. Store the file in S3 and pass URI to Redis queue and workers. 2. Make sure workers do not hold the whole file in memory rather read it through a stream. 3. Workers should do a proper cleanup after done processing or delegating the processing. 4. It seems that we would also benefit from putting final results in a file as well instead of keeping them in memory... (keeping partial results in concurrent workers in memory and returning them in memory is fine as long as we keep the batch not too big 1-10k)

Title: Changing an ID for an org, source, collection, etc.
Body: Currently, there is no process for replacing an ID for any OCL resource. How would we, for example, change this org ID from IHTSDO (which is outdated) to SNOMED-International? The process likely involves creating a new org and reloading the content, but users of that content would be impacted.

Title: Retire any APIs/data that use both repo version and resource version

Title: Highlight concept attributes/associations that were modified in the collection
Body: DESIGNS NEEDED BEFORE STARTING WORK ON THIS  When viewing concept details in the collection context, modifications made in the collection should be formatted / highlighted to indicate differences. Currently, we only allow modification of the display name and of associations, and we will support more modifications in the future.  See here for an example: https://api.qa.openconceptlab.org/orgs/OpenmrsTest/collections/test1/concepts/163361/106081/

Title: Constrain processing of repo versions to be "consistent" within an expansion
Body: Resolved repo version must be reused while evaluating the expansion to avoid the risk of an equivalent repo reference being evaluated differently while processing. For example, if I have several concept references to CIEL and a new version of CIEL is released while the expansions is being generated, then some of those concepts will point to an old version of CIEL and some to the new version of CIEL - we want to ensure that all of them point to the same repo version that was originally resolved  Processing method: * For each reference...   * Check if reference has already been resolved to a repo version     * If yes, use the "cached" repo version     * If no, resolve the reference to a repo version   * Evaluate the reference using the above repo version  NOTE: One ref can result in multiple repo versions (e.g. can include a system and one or more valuesets)

Title: Expose hard DELETE of concept/mapping for standard users
Body: Hard delete of concepts/mappings was implemented for admins only in #564. We want to be careful about how we expose this behavior to users, but we will eventually need to make this available. Let's use this ticket to track the issue.  @snyaggarwal Was hard delete implemented for concept/mapping versions?  ## Concept/mapping -- previously implemented ``` DELETE /orgs/MyOrg/sources/MySource/concepts/:id/?hardDelete=true DELETE /orgs/MyOrg/sources/MySource/mappings/:id/?hardDelete=true ```  ## Concept/mapping versions -- ?? ``` DELETE /orgs/MyOrg/sources/MySource/concepts/:id/:version/?hardDelete=true DELETE /orgs/MyOrg/sources/MySource/mappings/:id/:version/?hardDelete=true ```

Title: User should have option not to auto-import Mappings to retired concepts
Body: (@paynejd and @bmamlin do you agree with this?)  I propose: When a user goes through the "Add to Collection" workflow and wants all mapped concepts imported, e.g. as we often do with CIEL concepts, we should have the option (or perhaps this should be the default behavior) to NOT have mappings to retired concepts included.   Right now there's a mismatch: the *Mappings* to retired concepts are auto-imported, but the *retired concepts* themselves are _not_ auto-imported. I think this created for us the situation in https://github.com/OpenConceptLab/ocl_issues/issues/1395

Title: Implement maintenance page
Body: We need to be able to bring up a maintenance page for major upgrades or automatically if services are e.g. timing out due to bug or overload.  This should be available for api or web.  The maintenance page would ideally be served from CDN to survive traffic hikes.  We need to check if ALB on AWS is capable of providing such functionality or come up with a custom approach. It would be good to provide some basic alternative if running OCL with docker-compose in a form of e.g. nginx gateway, but can be addressed in a separate issue if it is more work.

Title: Deep linking
Body: **Goals** - URL in the web browser should generally update to current view throughout the TermBrowser - Copy/pasting the above URL should load the same view, even if user starts a new session, starts not signed in, etc. - Where the above isn't easy to accomplish, make it easy get a copy of a URL in the TermBrowser - Links where users may want to right click to copy a URL, the TermBrowser UI components should make this easy -- there are places now where this doesn't work - Ability to reference a specific synonym, attribute, mapping, etc. of a concept - While we're doing this, where a user may want to select and copy text (e.g. concept ID, name, mapping code/name/source), the TermBrowser UI components should make this easy -- there are some places now where this isn't easy because selecting the text results in some type of action  Do the above prioritizing key resources, including: - concept/mapping and concept/mapping within a source/collection version (specifically NOT prioritizing concept/mapping version) - source/collection and source/collection version - org - user  cc @bmamlin

Title: Dynamically populate display_name based on request header
Body: A concept's display name is populated based on the settings of the repository and the names available for a concept.  To get ready for multi-lingual capabilities, we want to add the ability to provide a language preference in the request header for a concept. The language preference will be considered (along with the repository settings and names available in a concept) when dynamically populating the concept display_name and display_locale. As part of this, we want the API to also indicate unambiguously which name/synonym was set as the display_name (e.g. by adding a display_name_id field?)  The default behavior (i.e. when no language preference is provided in the request header) will remain unchanged. Meaning, we can deploy this in production with no changes to the TermBrowser.  Steps to completion: - [ ] Summarize logic used to populate the display_name (here in the ticket is fine -- we'll add to the concept page in OCL Docs) - [ ] Draft updated logic for populating display_name to include language preference provided with a request - [ ] Update API endpoint(s) to accept the language preference request header - [ ] Implement new logic for populating display_name - [ ] Update ES index as required to support new behavior - [ ] Add `display_name_id` (or another attribute as determined) to unambiguously identify which name was used as the display name  Questions: - Is language preference only a single value or possibly a list?

Title: Use canonical URL where possible in the "system" field of a ValueSet

Title: Better handle invalid import JSON/CSV errors
Introduce "Synonym" name type and remove "None" name type
Body: Currently, OCL [name types](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/OCL/sources/NameTypes/) include `None` and do not have an entry for `Synonym`.  **Problem with `None`**: Since name type is optional, the difference between `None` and `null` is not defined and, as long as `None` exists, will cause headaches & confusion.  **`Synonym` as default**: an alternate name for a concept implies the very definition of [synonym](https://www.dictionary.com/browse/synonym), so it's natural to assume any name where name type is not specified is a synonym.  1. Purge the [None name type](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/OCL/sources/NameTypes/concepts/None/). Any existing data using the `None` name type can be converted to `null`.  2. Add a `Synonym` name type and start treating `null` & `Synonym` the same (i.e., assuming a name of unknown type is an alternate name ΓÇô a.k.a. synonym).  3. Optionally, once the codebase is treating `null` & `Synonym` the same, then we could elect to make name type required (converting all existing `null` entries to `Synonym`).

Title: Only create new concept/mapping version if the resource has changed
Body: Whether updating a single resource in the UI or the API, or performing a bulk import that is updating an existing repo (e.g. like CIEL), a concept or mapping's standard checksum should indicate whether or not the resource in the request represents a change. If there is a change, then the API should proceed as usual and attempt to update the resource (with no change to the response codes that are currently used). However if there is no change (i.e. the standard checksums are equal), then the API should not update the resource and it should return a different response code.  Which response code? - 409 conflict - 304 not modified  Neither of these seem quite right though because it's not an error or a redirect. I would think that it should be a 2** code, possibly with a response header to give more info?

Title: Concept tracing information - Modification indicators
Body: A user needs to be aware if the concept in a repo/collection that they are viewing is "original" (i.e. is the same as the source concept) or "modified" (i.e. its details, mappings, etc. differ from the source concept). In TBv2, this only manifests when the mappings of a concept differ from its original source vs. what is being displayed on the concept from the context of a collection. Complexities from this [OCL Arch Call](https://docs.google.com/document/d/157ChmfmYLGLEg_fO4cE7oMqtGpL4PchUkEhNfUUMxdo/edit#bookmark=id.pbgpc2ccacvm): - Demo concepts: [MSF concept](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/MSF/sources/MSF/concepts/1290/) (original from source) vs. [MSF-OCB concept](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/MSF-OCB/collections/Trauma/HEAD/expansions/autoexpand-HEAD/concepts/1290/) (modified mappings in collection) (see screenshots below) - Test case - when I add the concept from the MSF-OCB collection, I expect to see the concept and mappings from the MSF-OCB collection. Currently OCL points directly back to the original MSF sourceΓÇÖs mappings though. - Complexities: - ThereΓÇÖs a balance to strike between the dictionary management/auto-expand perspective vs. the traditional TS/FHIR/non-auto-expand perspective - When viewing the Concepts tab in the context of a collection/valueset, OCL currently displays: - Left: Codes/mappings from their codesystems/sources - If you check concept on the left and ΓÇ£add it to a collectionΓÇ¥, the reference points to the source, not the collection - Right: Concept from collection context (cascaded within context), and the link at the top of the details pane is to the source - Header and attributes are populated from source viewpoint - Display name can be set in a reference/valueset ΓÇô not clear if the TB shows the display name from the ValueSet, or if it only shows the info from the underlying source; but we know that the TB doesnΓÇÖt handle context or any other types of modifications that a user might eventually be able to make to a concept within a reference - ΓÇ£AssociationsΓÇ¥ section cascades from collection viewpoint Original MSF Concept: "Modified" MSF Concept:

Title: UI Method for adding collection-based reference to a collection
Body: Now that we worked on the API piece, we need a simple way to add a concept to a collection, giving the user a way to decide which content to use if applicable. This affects how cascade can resolve if the mappings to a concept differ between the source and the collection.                This is working well on Prod! Note that I only used the API method, and this doesn't cover anything in the UI yet. TB still doesn't have a great way to add a concept from the source's perspective vs. from the collection's perspective though. We can address that in another ticket.  _Originally posted by @jamlung-ri in https://github.com/OpenConceptLab/ocl_issues/issues/1764#issuecomment-1944038728_

Title: Implement standalone reference support in CSV bulk import
Body: We are missing support for a standalone reference row in the CSV bulk import format. For example, we want to support a CSV row that looks something like this (Note: this syntax still needs to be confirmed):  | resource_type | collection_url | data:expressions | | -- | -- | -- | | Reference | /orgs/mohlk/collections/HIMScloudPhysicalActivity/ | /orgs/mohlk/sources/HIMScloudSource/concepts/LK-PA-01/ |

One additional note: We will want to ensure that we open up user profiles more publicly, since I believe we currently cannot view other people's profiles, if I'm not mistaken?  > We dont have a verbose summary API for a user/org (which I think we should). >  > Right now in user summary we can get following: > - count of sources > - count of collections > - count of orgs membership > - count of orgs directly owned > - count of bookmarks  _Originally posted by @snyaggarwal in https://github.com/OpenConceptLab/ocl_issues/issues/1756#issuecomment-1960726098_

Title: Repo Version Summary counts are often incorrect
Body: Following large imports or large version export creations, this summary count of concepts and mappings is often incorrect, showing 0 of either resource or an otherwise incorrect number. This leads the user to having to use the Recalculate Summary button somewhat often.  Is there any quick fix that could be done, even if it means waiting to calculate the summary until later?  Screenshot where the issue is not occurring, but wanted to show which numbers I am talking about.  ![image](https://github.com/OpenConceptLab/ocl_issues/assets/55999682/8ff7865a-1cb8-42dc-8f67-bf938b617470)

Title: Draft checksum documentation (to go into OCL Docs)

Title: Create independent script for standard and smart checksums for concepts and mappings
Body: We want a script that can be run outside of the OCL core services. The script can be either python or javascript -- python might be easier for the moment. It must have the logic required to generate standard and smart checksums for concepts or mappings. The user should not need to know which fields should be included for standard/smart checksums -- this needs to be embedded in the script.  The initial goal is to enable @bmamlin to create checksums for CIEL resources outside of OCL to support validation workflows.  Also see #1691

Title: Enable creating/importing/editing resource in an older repo version
Body: **Challenge:** Version Model for resources and repo works on created_at. created_at is system generated datetime field, and cannot be tempered from outside. Whatever gets created later is the newer version.  **Tasks** 1. Need to establish a link between versions (linked list). Probably each versioned resource needs to have a URI of the previous    - `previous_version_uri` -- will this affect standard checksum?    - `version_order` -- for quick sorting 2. List versions -- Sort on all versions by `version_order` - 0 for initial version higher being newer 3. Latest version -- Sort on all versions by `version_order` and get max. 4. New version creation can have:    - `new_version.previous_version_uri=existing_latest_version.uri` and `new_version.version_order=existing_latest_version.version_order + 1` 5. In between version creation needs to be created with `previous_version_uri` in payload. (considering the `new_version` is added after a version say `previous_version`.     - `existing_next_version` is version where `versions.previous_version_uri=pervious_version_uri`    - `new_version.previous_version_uri=previous_version_uri` and `new_version.version_order=previous_version.version_order + 1`    - `existing_next_version.previous_version_uri=new_version.uri` and `existing_next_version.version_order=new_version.version_order + 1` 6. `previous_version_uri` and `version_order` cannot be edited from outside. 7. Latest version will have `is_latest_version=true` just like now for resources. For Repo we must add this to make it consistent and faster to lookup.

Title: v3 Search does not return private content
Body: When I search for a [private source on QA](https://app.v3.qa.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/private-org/sources/private-source/) from the v3 search, it doesn't appear. I can access the source directly from the organization, but the repo doesn't appear in global search.  Note that you can only tell the source is private from the v2 TermBrowser: https://app.qa.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/private-org/sources/private-source/ @paulsonder Should this privacy status appear in the Repository designs? e.g. https://zpl.io/eznn8ql - I assume yes, unless we are waiting for the Permissions Framework.   @snyaggarwal Is this an intentional decision or a bug?  ![Private source search result failure](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/095b7f03-23a3-4a6f-96f2-d0b9ad7863b9)

Title: Freeze large resources upon deletion request
Body: Deleting a large repo or org is pretty confusing for a user. There is no "Processing" flag. Content is not "frozen". They can continue to interact with the content while it is being processed asynchronously without any indication that it is being deleted.  Ideas for how we handle this: - Implement "Frozen" flag -- content that is frozen can only be viewed; the flag should be visible to users - When requesting a delete, OCL first "freezes" the content (assuming that this is a quick process?), and then kick off the deletion process  The idea is that "Freezing" content will be useful for other purposes: Loading a large repo; processing a large version; evaluating a large expansion; etc.  Finally, if I request a delete in TBv3, the UI should immediately respond, display a flag, etc.

Title: Concept Details Query Gone Wrong
Body: The concept details panel on the right is not showing the correct concept when clicking on a concept in the Mappings section. See the video below, where the user clicks on CIEL's concept 971, but instead of showing that concept, it shows concept 971 from the source that the user is working on. See "Aborto inevitable" appearing instead of CIEL's concept "Father", which should have appeared.  I was able to recreate this on OCL Online, both on the source in the video and in my own custom source. This appears to be a bug everywhere. TermBrowser seems to be directing the user to the source correctly, but the details pane is querying the wrong source.  **This is a high priority bug to fix**  Video: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f6fb9372-417b-43c8-80c2-023d2f325e22  Thanks to Alvaro Mendoza-Li for pointing this out!

Title: Collection Importing via Version Export
Body: While sources are able to successfully be transported between two OCL servers using export.json files, collection versions are not working in that way. When we try, OCL returns an error `"errors": {  "collection": "Version Head not found."   }`  A user should be able to use a collection version export JSON file from OCL to recreate that collection in another OCL server. Collection versions currently include references, so the MVP of this would be to create the collection (if needed) and then import the references from the collection version JSON.   Collection version JSON files do NOT include an expansion, but we can ignore that for now and just do an autoexpand.  A next stage for this might be to be responsive to the concepts and mappings in the collection version JSON, but that probably requires more discussion.

Body: <!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->  Why does the [newly created version](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/users/forest/sources/DemoFoods/25.04.08.01/) (the 1st one) of [my demo source](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/users/forest/sources/DemoFoods/) have a hierarchy different from the [HEAD](https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/users/forest/sources/DemoFoods) versionΓÇÖs although nothing is changed to the content?  Hierarchy of the version [HEAD]  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83693223-2dd8-405b-8df1-c807e5b202cb)  Hierarchy of the version 25.04.08.01  ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bbfd8c85-1ed1-4ba6-b71b-594961d8bfe9)  In other words, why would the hierarchy get messed up?  ## Expected Behavior <!--- Tell us what should happen --> **Comments by Joseph Amlung** The hierarchy should be appearing the same in both HEAD and in the version.  ## Current Behavior <!--- Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior --> The hierarchy would showed diferently in the HEAD version and the 1st version. **Comments by Joseph Amlung**  The hierarchy_root_url and hierarchy_meaning looks correct in both the HEAD and in the source version, and the concepts are both traversing down the hierarchy correctly.  ## Possible Solution <!--- Not obligatory, but suggest a fix/reason for the bug, --> **Comments by Sny Aggarwal**  I think this is a genuine bug in the UI.

Title: Mappings should persist repo URLs as submitted by the user
Body: When we did the major update to OCL on mappings and canonical URLs, our decision was that attributes must be recorded as the user submitted them in almost all cases. It is possible that we did not finish this work, but regardless, the from and to sources need to maintain a canonical URL if it is provided. Internally, we can (and should) _resolve_ the canonical URL to a relative URL, and we may (or may not) want to include that info in the response.  Determine the impact of this change across the oclapi2, oclweb2, oclweb3 and draft the implementation approach before proceeding.

Title: Cannot update user email address
Body: Currently, it isn't possible to update user email address. ## Expected Behavior User profile should be updated and new email address should be displayed instead of the old email address ## Current Behavior Saving the update profile form shows a success message but the email stays the same ## Possible Solution Possibly an issue with the update implementation not supporting it email change. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Login to OCL https://app.openconceptlab.org (successful login will open your profile) 2. Click on the `Edit Profile` button on the left section below the profile photo. That will open a workspace on the right where you can change your email address 3. After completing the edits, click the `Update` button. This action will show a success message on the top right showing success and the workspace will be closed automatically. 4. Check the left section to see if the email has changed. You will notice it stays the same. We've attempted this with `ocladmin` rights as well but the new email reverts to the old email. ## Context (Environment) ## Detailed Description ## Possible Implementation

Title: Dynamic reference cascade should account for equivalence to avoid false validation errors
Body: When adding a concept to a source with cascade, we check for equivalence. This means, when considering the addition of any concept X during the cascade, if an existing concept in the source is mapped SAME-AS concept X, we do not add concept X and any mappings for it are adjusted to point the equivalent concept in the source as they are added.  For example, if the CIEL concept "Home electricity?" is being added and the source already has concepts mapped SAME-AS to its "Yes" and "No" answers, the concept is added with mappings to existing "Yes" and "No" concepts within the source.  When dynamic references are added to a collection, custom validation rules are run against the result of merging the cascaded results with the collection (without considering equivalency and removing duplicates), causing false duplicate name validation errors.  Using the previous example, when trying to add the CIEL concept "Home electricity?" a collection as a dynamic reference, the validation schema is run against the combination of the collection's existing concepts and the cascade results, which includes "Yes" and "No" in the collection (mapped SAME-AS to CIEL equivalents) _and_ the "Yes" and "No" from CIEL, resulting in duplicate name errors.  Two possible solutions:  1. **Add equivalency logic to custom validation**. In the context of adding a dynamic reference to a collection, adapt custom validation to replicate the equivalency logic, so any instances of the same source + code or cases where an existing concept has a SAME-AS mapping to a candidate concept, the candidate and any children would be ignored. This has the downside of needing to ensure the logic for equivalency checking for custom validation is kept aligned with the equivalency checking used when expanding a collection.  2. **Add equivalency checking to cascading of the dynamic references**. Adapt the logic of cascading dynamic references to account for equivalency, so any concepts that already exist in the collection (same source+code or mapped SAME-AS to the candidate concept) are skipped and any mappings to them are adjusted to point to the collection's existing concept. This has the advantage that validation would be run against the outcome of cascading instead of an interim (un-deduplicated) state, but may be more challenging or have performance costs.

Title: "Add Reference" UI cascade options need to catch up with rest of TB
Body: We originally were letting this "Add References (from the collection)" UI lag behind because we were moving so much on TBv3, but we now are in a spot where we need to bring this UI up to date with the regular "Add to Collection" button that most people use. It's currently adding version numbers onto concepts/mappings that are added from this UI. This UI needs to not include the version numbers on concept and mapping references. Also, the concept/mapping query to a source version is using a parameter that makes it so that 0 concepts are returned. Example (where it seems that the excludeWildcard parameter is the issue?): https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/v2025-10-19/concepts/?excludeWildcard=true&excludeFuzzy=true&q=&page=1&limit=25&includeSearchMeta=true&verbose=false&sortDesc=_score

Title: Deleting Auto-expand HEAD for a collection
Body: OCL currently allows a user to delete their Auto-expand-HEAD expansion on their collection. There are a couple of actions that we need to take on this: 1. We should add a dialog box that warns the user about deleting their auto-expand HEAD. Basically an "are you sure about this" dialog. 2. If I already did delete my autoexpand-HEAD version, then I try to recreate it, it needs to have the "A" icon for auto-expand, since it's now effectively my official autoexpand-HEAD expansion for that collection. See screenshot below, where the "A" is missing because I deleted and "recreated" the autoexpand-HEAD expansion. It still acts like an autoexpand, but it doesn't seem to be the "official" one. Not sure if that has other behavioral impacts.

Title: Simplified CSV output that combines concepts and mappings
Body: OCL's export formats (Concepts CSV, Mappings CSV, and Export JSON) do not meet user requirements of having a full representation of a repository in a concept-focused way, particularly without limiting the number of resources to 1000.  We are exploring a new format that transforms OCL's export JSON into a concept-anchored CSV (meaning, each row is one concept, and those concepts' mappings are displayed in the same row).  A Python notebook to illustrate this work is here: https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1TH8j-95F1D19dgFpbMwhD1_A2eYRepGU?usp=sharing   Potential goals:  > In an ideal world, a new csv format would solve these problems simultaneously: >  * Export format from OCL >  * Compatible as an input dataset for OCL Mapper >  * Compatible with OCL's bulk import format >  * Very human-readable (Unlike the JSON format) >  * Comprehensive??? JSON represents 100% of concept metadata -- should this? >  * Enable representation of collection references?

Title: Automated migration script for resource version reference transformation
Body: ## Overview Develop an automated migration script to review all existing collection references and transform deprecated resource version references to valid reference models where possible.  ## Background Resource version references (format: `/:ownerType/:owner/sources/:source/concepts/:concept/:resourceVersion/`) are being deprecated in favor of: - **Version-less references**: `/:ownerType/:owner/sources/:source/concepts/:concept/` - **References with Repo Version**: `/:ownerType/:owner/sources/:source/:repoVersion/concepts/:concept/`  ## Script Requirements  ### Inputs (per reference) - The resource versioned reference being evaluated - Collection version's metadata and other references - Collection version's expansions  ### Transformation Logic  **Priority 1: Transform to version-less reference** - Referenced resource version is current HEAD/latest version - No locked/released expansion depends on specific resource version - Concept is not retired or inactive - No conflicting version references to same concept  **Priority 2: Transform to reference with Repo Version** - Collection version has expansion from specific source version - Resource version exists within that source version - Source version is still accessible - Use source version matching expansion resolution timestamp  **Flag for Manual Intervention** - Resource version not resolvable to HEAD or known source version - Concept retired/deleted (may be intentional for historical purposes) - Multiple valid source versions with no clear tiebreaker - Conflicting version references in same collection - Dependent mappings that wouldn't resolve correctly  ## Output - List of successfully transformed references (by type) - List of references requiring manual review with reasons - Confidence scores for transformations (if applicable) - Summary statistics  ## Related Issues Part of expansion versioning and reference transformation work (#2272-#2279)  ## Assignees @snyaggarwal @jamlung-ri

Title: Proposal to support download of a concept's mappings as a valueset
Body: ***THIS TICKET IS NOT READY FOR DEVELOPMENT YET***  Thanks to @delcroip for suggesting this feature  **Use Case:** CIEL:116128 has many mappings: https://app.openconceptlab.org/#/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/concepts/116128/. I want an endpoint to download those mappings (optionally with filtering, e.g. only SAME-AS) as a ValueSet that I can easily refer back to.  **MVP Proposal** - Implement a new OCL API endpoint with optional filtering (e.g. map-types, repo/source), e.g.: https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/concepts/116128/$getMappingsAsValueSet?mapTypes=SAME-AS,NARROWER-THAN,BROADER-THAN. This is a custom OCL API format -- we expect long-term that we would want a compliant FHIR method to request this, likely via an $expand request with a novel compose statement. - Return a minimalist FHIR-compliant ValueSet response of a conceptΓÇÖs mappings: ``` {   "name": "Mappings for CIEL:116128",   "url": "https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/concepts/116128/$getMappingsAsValueSet?mapTypes=SAME-AS,NARROWER-THAN,BROADER-THAN",   expansion: {     contains: []   } } ```

Title: Move OIDC client secret from frontend to backend (oclweb3 + oclapi2)
Body: ## Summary  The OIDC authorization code exchange flow currently requires the frontend (`oclweb3`) to hold and transmit the `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` to the backend. Per OAuth 2.0 best practices, confidential client secrets should only be stored and used server-side.  ## Current behavior  1. `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` is injected into browser-accessible JavaScript in two ways:    - At runtime via `env-config.js` (`window.OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET`) ΓÇö written by `start-prod.sh`    - At build time via webpack `DefinePlugin` (`process.env.OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET`) ΓÇö in `webpack.config.js` 2. The `OIDLoginCallback` component (`src/components/users/OIDLoginCallback.jsx`) reads the secret from the browser context and sends it in a POST request to the backend's `oidc/code-exchange/` endpoint 3. The backend (`core/users/views.py` ΓÇö `OIDCodeExchangeView`) receives `client_id` and `client_secret` from the request body and uses them to exchange the authorization code for a token  ## Expected behavior  The backend should store `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_ID` and `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` in its own environment and use them server-side during the code exchange. The frontend should only send `code` and `redirect_uri`.  ## Step 1: Ecosystem-wide audit (REQUIRED FIRST)  OCL Online is a complex ecosystem with many independent services and applications. Before making any code changes, a thorough audit must be conducted across the entire OCL Online ecosystem to identify **all** clients, applications, and services that handle OIDC client secrets in a similar way. This includes but is not limited to: - `oclweb3` and `oclweb2` (frontends) - `oclapi2` (backend) - Any other services that participate in OIDC flows (e.g. FHIR services, analytics, admin tools) - Docker compose configurations and deployment scripts across all environments - KeyCloak client registrations and their configurations  The goal is to ensure that all affected components are revised together, not just the ones identified so far.  ## Proposed changes (pending audit results)  **Backend (`oclapi2`):** - `core/settings.py`: Populate `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_ID` and `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` from environment variables (placeholder settings already exist at lines 564-582) - `core/users/views.py`: Update `OIDCodeExchangeView.post()` to read `client_id` / `client_secret` from `settings` instead of the request body - `core/integration_tests/tests_users.py`: Update `OIDCodeExchangeViewTest` to match new endpoint contract  **Frontend (`oclweb3`):** - `src/components/users/OIDLoginCallback.jsx`: Only send `code` and `redirect_uri` in the POST to `oidc/code-exchange/` - `webpack.config.js`: Remove `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` from `DefinePlugin` - `start-prod.sh`: Remove `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_SECRET` from `env-config.js` generation - `src/common/utils.js`: Update `isSSOEnabled()` to check only `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL` + `OIDC_RP_CLIENT_ID` (since the secret would no longer be in the frontend)  **Other services:** To be determined by the audit in Step 1.  ## Deployment note  Requires coordinated deployment: backend first (accept both old and new request formats during transition), then frontends/services, then remove backward compatibility from backend.  ## Labels  Security, oclweb3, oclapi2

Title: Mapping search: concept query param silently ignored on repo-scoped endpoint
Body: ## Summary  The `concept` query parameter on the mapping search endpoint (`GET /orgs/:org/sources/:source/mappings/?concept=116128`) is documented as filtering by "from or to concept" but is silently ignored. Results are identical to querying without the parameter.  `fromConcept` and `toConcept` work correctly. Only `concept` (the bidirectional filter) is broken.  ## Steps to Reproduce  ```bash # These return identical results ΓÇö concept param is silently ignored curl -s "https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/mappings/?concept=116128&limit=3" curl -s "https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/mappings/?limit=3"  # fromConcept and toConcept work correctly curl -s "https://api.openconceptlab.org/orgs/CIEL/sources/CIEL/mappings/?toConcept=116128&limit=3"  # returns 4 results ```  ## Root Cause  `concept` is not defined as a faceted field in `core/mappings/models.py` `es_fields`, so it gets dropped during `get_faceted_filters()` processing in `core/common/views.py`. The `fromConcept` and `toConcept` fields are properly defined as faceted, but there is no bidirectional `concept` equivalent.  ## Expected Behavior  `concept=116128` should return mappings where 116128 appears as either `from_concept_code` or `to_concept_code`.  ## Workaround  Use `fromConcept` and `toConcept` separately.

Title: Add X-OCL-CLIENT header to all OCL clients
Body: ## Summary  The OCL analytics system already supports client identification via the `X-OCL-CLIENT` request header (whitelisted in `AnalyticsEventEmitter`, extracted into `request.client` field). However, no OCL client currently sends it ΓÇö all rely on imprecise fallback auto-detection from User-Agent/Origin headers.  ## Clients to update  | Client | Header value | Status | |---|---|---| | ocl-cli | `ocl-cli/{version}` | Γ£à Done | | oclweb3 | `oclweb3/{version}` | Pending | | oclweb2 | `oclweb2/{version}` | Pending | | oclmap | `oclmap/{version}` | Pending | | ocl-mcp | `ocl-mcp/{version}` | Pending | | ocl-ai-assistant | `ocl-ai-assistant/{version}` | Pending | | ocl-scispacy-loinc | `ocl-scispacy-loinc/{version}` | Pending |  Each client adds `X-OCL-CLIENT: {app-name}/{version}` to all outgoing API requests. Python clients also add a descriptive `User-Agent`. No changes needed on the analytics or API server side.  ## Related - See also: follow-up ticket to inject version from build config in JS frontends (webpack DefinePlugin)

Title: Inject X-OCL-CLIENT version from build config in JS frontends
Body: ## Summary  The JS frontends (oclweb3, oclweb2, oclmap) currently hardcode their version in the `X-OCL-CLIENT` header. This should be wired through webpack DefinePlugin so the version is injected from package.json at build time, avoiding manual updates at release time.  ## Approach  Use webpack's `DefinePlugin` to expose `process.env.APP_VERSION` from each project's `package.json` version field, then reference it in `APIService.js` `getHeaders()`.
